case_title: JUAN GARCIA VS. URBAN CONSTRUCTION CORP. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION

docket_entries.description: REQUEST THAT CLERK ENTER JUDGMENT ON FINAL ORDER, DECISION OR AWARD OF THE LABOR COMMISSIONER FILED BY PLAINTIFF GARCIA, JUAN AS TO DEFENDANT URBAN CONSTRUCTION CORP. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED

UNDER APPLICATION TO THE CLERK FOR JUDGMENT UNDER SEC 585(1) C.C.P., IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF GARCIA, JUAN RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT URBAN CONSTRUCTION CORP. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION $5,024.60 PRINCIPAL, $192.29 INTEREST, $205.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $5,421.89, SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT
SUBSTITUTION OF ATTORNEY FILED BY PLAINTIFF GARCIA, JUAN

NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO AMEND THE JUDGMENT TO AND ADD NON PARTY ALATER EGO, JOHN ZE-FENG LOWE, AS JUDGMENT DEBTOR FILED BY PLAINTIFF GARCIA, JUAN HEARING SET FOR APR-17-2012 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302
MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FILED BY PLAINTIFF GARCIA, JUAN
DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FILED BY PLAINTIFF GARCIA, JUAN

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF JUAN GARCIA'S MOTION TO AMEND THE JUDGMENT TO ADD NON PARTY ALTER EGO, JOHN ZE-FENG LOWE, AS JUDGMENT DEBTOR IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO BEING RE-FILED. THERE IS NO SHOWING THAT THE PROPOSED ALTER EGO WAS PERSONALLY SERVED. MOREOVER, THERE IS AN INSUFFICIENT EVIDENTIARY SHOWING TO SUPPORT THE MOTION. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.
MINI MINUTES FOR APR-17-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
ABSTRACT OF JUDGMENT ISSUED TO PLAINTIFF GARCIA, JUAN

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend The Judgment To And Add Non Party Alater Ego, John Ze-Feng Lowe, As Judgment Debtor
Off Calendar Apr-17-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court modified its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF JUAN GARCIA'S Motion To Amend The Judgment To Add Non Party Alter Ego, John Ze-Feng Lowe, As Judgment Debtor is Denied WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO BEING RE-FILED. There is no showing that the proposed alter ego was personally served. Moreover, there is an insufficient evidentiary showing to support the motion. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.
